mappings.name: parent
mappings.desc: (Term id) Parent term to create a group

mappings.name: synonyms
mappings.desc: Comma-separated alternative names for this term

terms.name: UNCERTAINTY
terms.description: Lack of knowledge or certainty about the future state of the variables that produce an outcome. Not all uncertainties cause risk – some may create opportunities instead.

terms.name: ASSUMPTION
terms.description: A proposition accepted as true or as certain to occur, without proof. It serves as a logical underpinning of the organization’s objectives, though not necessarily in a direct or obvious manner. It may also function as an expression of risk appetite.

terms.name: RISK
terms.description: An effect of one or more uncertainties on an objective. It is a set of possible consequences arising from deviations from, or violations of, assumptions.

terms.name: RecurringRisk
terms.description: A risk characterized by a chronic condition that repeatedly triggers discrete loss events over time. The events are distinguishable and countable, but their recurrence is systematic, indicating an unresolved underlying cause.

terms.name: Tolerance
terms.description: Tolerance is an acceptance envelope — the range of deviation from a desired state that a system or agent is willing to absorb without initiating a response. It encodes a relational stance: how much imperfection or uncertainty the agent will bear before acting. Tolerance is inherently normative and often internally defined — it reflects values, risk appetite, or resource constraints.

terms.name: PersistentRisk
terms.description: A risk characterized by a structural condition that continuously generates adverse outcomes without discrete triggering events. The harm is not episodic but inherent to the condition itself, enduring as long as the condition remains unaddressed.

terms.name: CompoundRisk
terms.description: Not a risk per se, but an statistical aggregation of individual singular risks.

terms.name: ACTION
terms.description: The practical implementation of a decision. It is dependent on resources, technical viability, and cultural acceptance.

terms.name: OBSERVATION
terms.description: The result of proactive attention to relevant evidence and its subsequent recording. It serves as a reality check and confirmation of consistency within a system.

terms.name: DECISION
terms.description: A resolution on whether to take an action, based on prior inputs.

terms.name: POLICY
terms.description: Specification of how a decision should be made, including constraints and criteria.

terms.name: OBJECTIVE
terms.description: Long-term goal tied to the organization's mission and vision, driving overall direction.

terms.name: FrequencyControl
terms.description: An intervention that aims to reduce (or increase) the frequency of events.

terms.name: ImpactControl
terms.description: An intervention that aims to reduce (or increase) the consequence of events.

terms.name: Threshold
terms.description: Threshold is a boundary condition — a point in a continuum where the system transitions from one state to another. It is positional and discrete in its effect: you are either below it or above it, and crossing it triggers a qualitative change (an alarm fires, a classification shifts, an action is mandated). It describes the structure of the decision space, not the disposition of the agent making the decision. Thresholds are often externally defined — by regulators, physics, or design.

terms.name: Requirement
terms.description: An aspect of a law, regulation or internal norm that must be followed. It may be qualitative or quantitative.

terms.name: INDICATOR
terms.description: A consolidation of observations, ideally complemented by contextual interpretation. It provides information useful for comparing the current state of a relevant variable with its desired or forecasted state. It may function just for monitoring, or as a warning or an insight.
